notes: The revised criteria sufficiently distinguish fixture-mandated requirements from golden-example implementation choices by marking exact template, reference, script, and sync command names as examples unless explicitly required.

The revised criteria provide usable evaluator guidance for unavailable token cost, wall-clock, and stability metadata, and defer those measurements to CLI telemetry or rerun evidence instead of failing first-pass candidates.
The revised criteria explicitly penalize unsupported claims about created files, local sync, smoke execution, and release readiness unless direct execution evidence is present.

The quality gate is now bounded around public-safety, author_skill_package lane completeness, evidence-versus-assumption separation, runtime-versus-tracked ownership, and false execution claim prevention.
